annotations.label: p-adic number (series form)

anchor.snippet: a p -adic number can be defined as a series

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Padic` as the Cauchy-sequence completion of ℚ with respect to `padicNorm`, not as formal series of `a_i p^i`, so the series-form definition is not directly formalized.

annotations.label: p-adic integer

anchor.snippet: A p -adic integer is a p -adic number such that

annotations.note: `PadicInt p := {x : ℚ_[p] // ‖x‖ ≤ 1}` directly formalizes p-adic integers as elements of ℚ_[p] with norm ≤ 1, equivalent to non-negative valuation.

annotations.label: p-adic absolute value

anchor.snippet: it is convergent for the p -adic absolute value

annotations.note: `padicNorm p q` on ℚ and the lifted `padicNormE` / `Norm` instance on ℚ_[p] formalize the p-adic absolute value.

annotations.label: Unique p-adic series representation of rationals

anchor.snippet: Every rational number can be uniquely expressed as the sum of a series as above

annotations.note: No theorem in Mathlib states that every rational is uniquely a convergent p-adic series of digits in {0,…,p−1}; only Cauchy-sequence representatives and approximations via `appr` exist.

annotations.label: Reduction mod p is not injective

anchor.snippet: doing this loses some information because the reduction

annotations.note: No specific statement in Mathlib formalizes 'reduction mod p loses information' as a motivational fact for p-adics.

annotations.label: p-adic numbers form a field

anchor.snippet: the set of p -adic numbers

annotations.note: `Padic p` is declared with `deriving … Field` (line 533 of PadicNumbers.lean), so ℚ_[p] is a field instance in Mathlib.

annotations.label: p-adic integers as base-p expansions

anchor.snippet: p -adic integers can be thought of as integers in base- p , but the digits extend infinitely to the left

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define `PadicInt` via formal base-p digit expansions; only the `appr` function gives finite truncations as natural numbers.

annotations.label: Ring of p-adic integers

anchor.snippet: Since addition and multiplication can be performed with p -adic integers, they form a ring

annotations.note: `instance instCommRing : CommRing ℤ_[p]` (line 92) directly establishes the ring structure on p-adic integers.

annotations.label: p-adic number as p-adic integer with finitely many fractional digits

anchor.snippet: p -adic numbers can be thought of as p -adic integers with finitely many digits after the decimal point

annotations.note: Mathlib proves `ℚ_[p]` is the fraction ring of `ℤ_[p]` (`PadicInt.isFractionRing`), formalizing the algebraic content of 'p-adic numbers are p-adic integers with finitely many fractional digits' without the digit-expansion form.

annotations.label: p-adic numbers form a field

anchor.snippet: the p -adic numbers form a field

annotations.note: Same as the Motivation field statement: `Padic p` derives `Field` in PadicNumbers.lean.

annotations.label: 1/5 as a 3-adic integer

anchor.snippet: the rational number ⁠ 1 / 5 ⁠ is a 3-adic integer

annotations.note: No specific example in Mathlib establishes 1/5 as a 3-adic integer, though it follows from `PadicInt.isUnit_den` applied to a representative.

annotations.label: p-adic integer as compatible sequence of residues

anchor.section: As a sequence of residues mod p k
anchor.snippet: Another way to define p -adic integers is by representing it as a sequence of residues

annotations.note: Mathlib provides the compatible system of maps `PadicInt.toZModPow n : ℤ_[p] →+* ZMod (p^n)` and a universal `lift`, but does not define `ℤ_[p]` itself as a compatible-residue sequence.

annotations.label: Equivalence of base-p and residue definitions

anchor.section: As a sequence of residues mod p k
anchor.snippet: This is equivalent to the base- p definition, because the last k digits of a base- p expansion uniquely define its value mod p k

annotations.note: Since Mathlib never sets up the base-p digit definition, no equivalence proof between digit and residue definitions exists.

annotations.label: 1/5 as 3-adic integer via residues

anchor.section: As a sequence of residues mod p k
anchor.snippet: its 3-adic expansion consists of the multiplicative inverses of 5 mod 3

annotations.note: No worked example computing the 3-adic expansion of 1/5 via inverses mod 3^k exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: p-adic integer (formal power series)

anchor.section: As formal series in base p
anchor.snippet: A p -adic integer is often defined as a formal power series of the form

annotations.note: Mathlib's `PadicInt` is the unit ball in ℚ_[p], not a formal power series ring with digit coefficients in {0,…,p−1}.

annotations.label: p-adic unit

anchor.section: As formal series in base p
anchor.snippet: A p -adic unit is a p -adic integer whose first digit is nonzero

annotations.note: `PadicInt.isUnit_iff` characterizes units of ℤ_[p] as elements of norm 1, equivalent (via `toZMod`) to having nonzero leading digit.

annotations.label: p-adic number (formal Laurent series)

anchor.section: As formal series in base p
anchor.snippet: A p -adic number is then defined as a formal Laurent series of the form

annotations.note: Mathlib's ℚ_[p] is a Cauchy completion of ℚ, not a formal Laurent series construction.

annotations.label: p-adic valuation

anchor.section: As formal series in base p
anchor.snippet: The first index v for which the digit

annotations.note: `Padic.valuation : ℚ_[p] → ℤ` (line 1040) gives the p-adic valuation; `padicValRat`, `padicValInt`, `padicValNat` provide it on ℚ/ℤ/ℕ.

annotations.label: Field of p-adic numbers

anchor.section: As formal series in base p
anchor.snippet: With these operations, the set of p -adic numbers form a field

annotations.note: `Padic p` is given a `Field` instance via the `deriving` clause in its definition.

annotations.label: Factorization lemma for rationals

anchor.snippet: Every nonzero rational number r can be written

annotations.note: Mathlib has `padicValRat` and many divisibility lemmas implying the factorization r = p^v · m/n with p coprime to mn, but no single named lemma packages the canonical factorization.

annotations.label: p-adic valuation of a rational

anchor.snippet: The exponent v is uniquely determined by r and is called its p -adic valuation

annotations.note: `padicValRat p q : ℤ` directly formalizes the p-adic valuation of a rational number.

annotations.label: p-adic series

anchor.snippet: A p -adic series is a formal Laurent series of the form

annotations.note: Mathlib uses Cauchy sequences (`PadicSeq`) rather than formal Laurent series in p with integer coefficients; no `PadicSeries` type exists.

annotations.label: Equivalence of p-adic series

anchor.snippet: are equivalent if there is an integer N such that

annotations.note: Since p-adic series are not defined, neither is the equivalence relation between them; Mathlib instead uses CauSeq equivalence under `padicNorm`.

annotations.label: Normalized p-adic series

anchor.snippet: is normalized if either all

annotations.note: No notion of 'normalized p-adic series' (with digits in {0,…,p−1}) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of normalized series

anchor.snippet: Every p -adic series is equivalent to exactly one normalized series

annotations.note: Without a notion of normalized series, the uniqueness theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Series operations compatible with equivalence

anchor.snippet: The usual operations of series (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) are compatible with equivalence of p -adic series

annotations.note: Operations on p-adic series and their compatibility with equivalence are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: p-adic numbers as equivalence classes

anchor.snippet: the p -adic numbers are defined as the equivalence classes of p -adic series

annotations.note: Mathlib defines ℚ_[p] as equivalence classes of `PadicSeq p` (Cauchy sequences of rationals under `padicNorm`), morally equivalent but not via formal series.

annotations.label: p-adic absolute value (equivalence-class form)

anchor.snippet: The p -adic absolute value of a nonzero p -adic number x

annotations.note: `padicNormE` and the norm instance on ℚ_[p] are defined as |x|_p = p^(-v(x)), formalizing the absolute value via the valuation.

annotations.label: Field of p-adic numbers (via normalized series)

anchor.snippet: p -adic numbers form a field called the field of p -adic numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Padic p` is the field of p-adic numbers (constructed differently from normalized series).

annotations.label: Unique homomorphism from rationals to p-adics

anchor.snippet: There is a unique field homomorphism from the rational numbers into the p -adic numbers

annotations.note: The rational embedding `Rat.cast : ℚ → ℚ_[p]` exists with dense range and is a ring hom; uniqueness from ℚ follows from `Rat`'s initial-field property but no named lemma states it explicitly for ℚ_[p].

annotations.label: Valuation as discrete valuation

anchor.snippet: This valuation is a discrete valuation

annotations.note: `instance : IsDiscreteValuationRing ℤ_[p]` (line 521) formalizes that the p-adic valuation is a discrete valuation.

annotations.label: Division step for p-adic expansion

anchor.snippet: The division step consists of writing

annotations.note: The explicit division-step algorithm for p-adic expansions of rationals is not formalized.

annotations.label: Computing a via modular inverse

anchor.snippet: The integer a can be computed as a modular multiplicative inverse

annotations.note: No formalization of computing p-adic digits via modular inverses is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: p-adic expansion is eventually periodic iff rational

anchor.snippet: The p -adic expansion of a rational number is eventually periodic

annotations.note: The 'eventually periodic iff rational' characterization of p-adic expansions is not formalized.

annotations.label: 5-adic expansion computation

anchor.snippet: Let us compute the 5-adic expansion of

annotations.note: No specific worked example computing a 5-adic expansion is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: p-adic integers as nonnegative-valuation p-adics

anchor.snippet: The p -adic integers are the p -adic numbers with a nonnegative valuation

annotations.note: `PadicInt p := {x : ℚ_[p] // ‖x‖ ≤ 1}` is equivalent to nonnegative valuation, with `valuation_coe_nonneg` providing the link.

annotations.label: Rationals that are p-adic integers

anchor.snippet: The rational numbers of the form

annotations.note: `PadicInt.isUnit_den` shows that a rational r has ‖r‖_p ≤ 1 iff its denominator is a p-adic unit (i.e., not divisible by p), exactly the rationals that are p-adic integers.

annotations.label: Z_p is an integral domain

anchor.snippet: It is an integral domain , since it is a subring of a field

annotations.note: `instance : IsDomain ℤ_[p]` (line 204) directly establishes that ℤ_[p] is an integral domain.

annotations.label: Units of Z_p

anchor.snippet: The units (invertible elements) of

annotations.note: `PadicInt.isUnit_iff : IsUnit z ↔ ‖z‖ = 1` characterizes the units of ℤ_[p] as norm-1 elements.

annotations.label: Z_p is a principal ideal domain

anchor.snippet: It is a principal ideal domain , such that each ideal is generated by a power of p

annotations.note: `PadicInt.ideal_eq_span_pow_p` shows every nonzero ideal is `span {p^n}`; together with the DVR instance this gives the PID structure.

annotations.label: Z_p is a local ring of Krull dimension one

anchor.snippet: It is a local ring of Krull dimension one

annotations.note: `instance : IsLocalRing ℤ_[p]` (line 499) formalizes the local-ring part; Krull dimension one follows from the DVR instance but no explicit `krullDim = 1` statement was located.

annotations.label: Z_p is a discrete valuation ring

anchor.snippet: It is a discrete valuation ring , since this results from the preceding properties

annotations.note: `instance : IsDiscreteValuationRing ℤ_[p]` (line 521) establishes the DVR structure.

annotations.label: Z_p as completion of localization

anchor.snippet: It is the completion of the local ring

annotations.note: `PadicInt.adicCompletionIntegersEquiv` and `IsAdicComplete (maximalIdeal ℤ_[p]) ℤ_[p]` together encode that ℤ_[p] is the (p)-adic completion of ℤ_(p) / ℤ, matching the textbook statement.

annotations.label: p-adic absolute value via valuation

anchor.snippet: the p -adic absolute value of a nonzero p -adic number x is

annotations.note: `Padic.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation` proves ‖x‖ = p^(-valuation x) for nonzero x ∈ ℚ_[p].

annotations.label: Strong triangle inequality

anchor.snippet: This is an absolute value that satisfies the strong triangle inequality

annotations.note: `Padic.nonarchimedean : ‖q + r‖ ≤ max ‖q‖ ‖r‖` (line 839) directly states the strong triangle inequality.

annotations.label: p-adic distance and ultrametric

anchor.snippet: This makes the p -adic numbers a metric space , and even an ultrametric space

annotations.note: `instance : IsUltrametricDist ℚ_[p]` (line 781) plus `MetricSpace ℚ_[p]` formalize the ultrametric structure.

annotations.label: Q_p as completion of Q

anchor.snippet: the p -adic numbers form the completion of the rational numbers equipped with the p -adic absolute value

annotations.note: By definition `Padic p := CauSeq.Completion.Cauchy (padicNorm p)`, together with `Padic.rat_dense` and `CompleteSpace ℚ_[p]`, formalizing the completion property.

annotations.label: Open balls equal closed balls

anchor.snippet: As the metric is defined from a discrete valuation , every open ball is also closed

annotations.note: General ultrametric facts about clopen balls live in `IsUltrametricDist` API, which applies to ℚ_[p] via its `IsUltrametricDist` instance; no Padic-specific 'open = closed ball' lemma was found.

annotations.label: Q_p locally compact, Z_p compact

anchor.snippet: form a locally compact space

annotations.note: `PadicInt.compactSpace : CompactSpace ℤ_[p]` plus `instance : ProperSpace ℚ_[p]` (giving local compactness) formalize both claims.

annotations.label: Z_p homeomorphic to Cantor set

anchor.snippet: The space of 2-adic integers

annotations.note: No homeomorphism between ℤ_[2] and the Cantor set is established in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pontryagin dual of Z_p

anchor.snippet: The Pontryagin dual of the group of p -adic integers is the Prüfer p -group

annotations.note: Mathlib has no PrüferGroup type and no theorem identifying the Pontryagin dual of ℤ_[p] with the Prüfer p-group.

annotations.label: Z_p / p^n Z_p ≅ Z/p^n Z

anchor.snippet: may be identified with the ring

annotations.note: `PadicInt.toZModPow n : ℤ_[p] →+* ZMod (p^n)` with `ker_toZModPow` showing the kernel is `span {p^n}` formalizes the isomorphism ℤ_[p]/p^n ≅ ℤ/p^nℤ.

annotations.label: Inverse limit of Z/p^n

anchor.snippet: The inverse limit of the rings

annotations.note: Mathlib does not name the inverse limit of `ZMod (p^n)` explicitly, but `PadicInt.lift` proves ℤ_[p] satisfies the universal property of that inverse limit.

annotations.label: Z_p as inverse limit

anchor.snippet: The mapping that maps a normalized p -adic series to the sequence of its partial sums is a ring isomorphism

annotations.note: `PadicInt.lift_unique` shows ℤ_[p] is the universal/limit ring for compatible systems of `ZMod (p^n)`-valued ring homs, formalizing 'ℤ_[p] = lim ZMod (p^n)'.

annotations.label: Newton's method for p-adic inverse

anchor.snippet: for computing the p -adic (multiplicative) inverse of an integer, one can use Newton's method

annotations.note: No Newton's-method procedure for computing p-adic inverses is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Newton's method for p-adic square root

anchor.snippet: The same method can be used for computing the p -adic square root of an integer

annotations.note: No Newton's-method computation of p-adic square roots is formalized.

annotations.label: Hensel lifting of polynomial factorization

anchor.snippet: Hensel lifting is a similar method that allows to

annotations.note: `hensels_lemma` lifts approximate roots in ℤ_[p] to exact ones (root form of Hensel), but the factorization form of Hensel lifting is not formalized as a separate theorem.

annotations.label: Cardinality of Q_p and Z_p

anchor.snippet: are uncountable and have the cardinality of the continuum

annotations.note: No theorem stating `#ℚ_[p] = 𝔠` or `#ℤ_[p] = 𝔠` was found in Mathlib (loogle returns 0 hits for Padic ∩ cardinal).

annotations.label: Q_p has characteristic 0

anchor.snippet: is a field of characteristic 0

annotations.note: `instance : CharZero ℚ_[p]` (line 563) directly establishes characteristic 0.

annotations.label: Q_p not orderable

anchor.snippet: cannot be turned into an ordered field

annotations.note: No statement that ℚ_[p] cannot be linearly ordered as a field was found.

annotations.label: Algebraic closure of Q_p has infinite degree

anchor.snippet: the algebraic closure of

annotations.note: No theorem about the degree (infinite) of the algebraic closure of ℚ_[p] is present; only the existence of `PadicAlgCl p` is formalized.

annotations.label: C_p is algebraically closed

anchor.snippet: is algebraically closed

annotations.note: `instance isAlgClosed : IsAlgClosed ℂ_[p]` (line 244) directly formalizes that ℂ_[p] is algebraically closed.

annotations.label: C_p not locally compact

anchor.snippet: this field is not locally compact

annotations.note: No theorem stating ℂ_[p] is not locally compact was found.

annotations.label: C_p isomorphic to C

anchor.snippet: are isomorphic

annotations.note: The (noncanonical, AC-dependent) isomorphism ℂ_[p] ≅ ℂ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Absolute Galois group prosolvable

anchor.snippet: the absolute Galois group

annotations.note: No statement about the absolute Galois group of ℚ_[p] being prosolvable (Iwasawa) is formalized.

annotations.label: Torsion subgroup of Q_p*

anchor.snippet: Hensel's lemma implies that the torsion subgroup of

annotations.note: No theorem identifying the torsion subgroup of ℚ_[p]^× (e.g., as (p−1)-th roots of unity for odd p) is formalized.

annotations.label: Cyclotomic subfields of Q_p

anchor.snippet: contains the n th cyclotomic field if and only if

annotations.note: No statement classifying cyclotomic subfields inside ℚ_[p] is present. Anchor tightened from generic 'the field' to a unique phrase pointing at the actual claim.

annotations.label: Finite index of k-th powers

anchor.snippet: Given a natural number k , let

annotations.note: No theorem about the finite index of (ℚ_[p]^×)^k in ℚ_[p]^× is present.

annotations.label: Hasse's local–global principle

anchor.snippet: Helmut Hasse 's local–global principle is said to hold for an equation if it can be solved over the rational numbers if and only if it can be solved over the real numbers and over the p -adic numbers for every prime p

annotations.note: Hasse–Minkowski / local-global principle is not formalized in Mathlib (no matches for Hasse-Minkowski).

annotations.label: Mahler's theorem

anchor.snippet: Mahler's theorem , which characterizes every continuous p -adic function in terms of polynomials

annotations.note: `PadicInt.hasSum_mahler` and the isometric equivalence `PadicInt.mahlerEquiv` together state Mahler's theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Completion of Dedekind domain at prime

anchor.snippet: Suppose D is a Dedekind domain and E is its field of fractions

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the adic completion of a Dedekind domain at a height-one prime via `IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion`, used in `Padic.adicCompletionEquiv` to recover ℚ_[p].

annotations.label: Ostrowski's theorem for number fields

anchor.snippet: Ostrowski's theorem says that every non-trivial non-Archimedean absolute value on E arises as some

annotations.note: Ostrowski's theorem (classifying absolute values on number fields) does not appear to be formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: p-adic solenoids

anchor.snippet: p -adic integers can be extended to p -adic solenoids

annotations.note: p-adic solenoids are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Profinite integers

anchor.snippet: The p -adic integers can also be extended to profinite integers

annotations.note: Profinite integers (Ẑ) are not defined as a named object in Mathlib.

annotations.label: n-adic integers via Chinese remainder theorem

anchor.snippet: The Chinese remainder theorem likewise implies a structure of

annotations.note: No 'n-adic integer' construction with CRT-decomposition into products of ℤ_[p_i] is formalized.

annotations.label: Hensel's lemma lifts solutions mod p to higher prime powers

anchor.snippet: applying Hensel's lemma to lift solutions modulo p to modulo p 2 , p 3

annotations.note: `hensels_lemma` in Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.Hensel produces an exact root in ℤ_[p] from a sufficiently good approximate solution, which is the lifting statement.

annotations.label: Quote notation for p-adic rationals

anchor.snippet: Quote notation is a variant of the p -adic representation of rational numbers

annotations.note: Quote notation for p-adic rationals is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Teichmüller representatives as p-adic digits

anchor.snippet: Teichmüller representatives are sometimes used as digits

annotations.note: While Mathlib has Witt-vector Teichmüller maps elsewhere, no formalization of Teichmüller representatives as p-adic integer digits was located in the Padics directory.

annotations.label: R has a unique proper algebraic extension

anchor.snippet: The field of real numbers

annotations.note: No theorem in Mathlib states uniqueness of ℂ as the proper algebraic extension of ℝ; only `Complex.isAlgClosed` is present.

annotations.label: Complex p-adic numbers C_p

anchor.snippet: Its (metric) completion is denoted

annotations.note: `PadicComplex p` (notation ℂ_[p]) is defined as `UniformSpace.Completion (PadicAlgCl p)`, exactly the metric completion of the algebraic closure of ℚ_[p].

annotations.label: Volkenborn integral

anchor.snippet: Volkenborn integral , which provides a method of integration for p -adic functions

annotations.note: The Volkenborn integral is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: p-adic Hodge theory

anchor.snippet: p -adic Hodge theory is a theory that provides a way to classify and study p -adic Galois representations

annotations.note: p-adic Hodge theory is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: p-adic Teichmüller theory

anchor.snippet: p -adic Teichmüller theory describes the

annotations.note: p-adic Teichmüller theory is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: p-adic quantum mechanics

anchor.snippet: p -adic quantum mechanics is a collection of related research efforts in quantum physics that replace real numbers with p -adic numbers

annotations.note: p-adic quantum mechanics is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Adele rings and idele groups

anchor.snippet: This is accomplished by adele rings and idele groups

annotations.note: Mathlib has adic completions and infrastructure used to build adele/idele rings in `Mathlib.NumberTheory.NumberField.AdeleRing` and similar, but the exact 'adele/idele' definitions as a single decl were not verified in the Padics directory; treated as partial.

annotations.label: Q_p as fraction field of completion of localization of Z at (p)

anchor.snippet: the field of the p -adic numbers is the field of fractions of the completion of the localization of the integers

annotations.note: `Padic.adicCompletionEquiv` identifies ℚ_[p] with the (p)-adic completion of ℚ; together with `PadicInt.adicCompletionIntegersEquiv` this exactly formalizes 'ℚ_[p] = Frac(completion of ℤ_(p))'.
